purpose.heading: What is ChatGPT for?
paragraphs: ChatGPT is OpenAI's flagship conversational AI, designed to make AI accessible to everyone through natural conversation. It serves two distinct masters simultaneously.
For Users: A versatile thinking partner that helps people write, learn, strategise, create, and solve problems - across every domain of life and work. No technical knowledge required.

For OpenAI: The primary consumer product driving adoption, revenue, and data at scale. ChatGPT is both the front door to OpenAI's ecosystem and the proof of concept that conversational AI can be mainstream.

personas.title: Knowledge Workers
personas.description: Professionals who use ChatGPT daily for drafting, summarising, researching, and problem-solving across every domain of work.

personas.title: Students & Learners
personas.description: The fastest-growing segment. ChatGPT is used as a tutor, study companion, and on-demand explainer for complex topics.

personas.title: Developers
personas.description: Use ChatGPT for code generation, debugging, and documentation. A strong segment, though increasingly contested by Claude and Copilot.

personas.title: Enterprise Teams
personas.description: Adopt ChatGPT for productivity at scale - drafting, summarising, and automating internal workflows. Still navigating compliance and governance concerns.

personas.title: Creators & Marketers
personas.description: Use ChatGPT for ideation, content drafts, social copy, and campaign brainstorming. Value its speed and breadth over depth.

jtbd: Think out loud without judgement, at any hour
Get a first draft, a framework, or a sanity check fast
Learn something new without sitting through a course
Generate and iterate on creative content at speed
Automate repetitive writing and research tasks

what_works.title: Breadth of Knowledge
points: ChatGPT covers more domains more confidently than any other AI assistant.
From cooking to quantum physics, legal drafts to Python scripts - one interface handles it all.
No technical knowledge required. The most accessible AI product ever built.

what_works.title: Personalisation & Memory
points: ChatGPT remembers facts, preferences, and context across conversations.
Over time, it adapts its tone and output to match how a user communicates.
The most advanced memory system among consumer AI products today.

what_works.title: Multimodal Capability
points: Text, images, voice, video, code, files - ChatGPT handles them all in one conversation.
Advanced Voice Mode allows real-time spoken conversation with low latency.
Image generation via DALL-E is natively integrated, no tool-switching required.

what_works.title: Ease of Use
points: The simplest onboarding of any AI product. Open, type, get value.
Available on web, mobile, and desktop with seamless cross-device continuity.
The brand recognition alone drives adoption - ChatGPT is synonymous with AI for most users.

problems.heading: What problems exist today?
problems.intro: Understanding the gaps across each stakeholder reveals where the product is falling short - and where the real opportunity lies.

stakeholders.name: End Users
points: Memory is surface level: remembers facts you share, but stops short of building a true model of how you think and decide.
Over-validation: agrees even when users are confidently wrong
No emotional intelligence signals: doesn't detect when a user needs to slow down
Trust without verification: users can't easily fact-check outputs

stakeholders.name: Enterprise Users
points: Data privacy concerns: inputs may train future models
No audit trail or response tracing: hard to govern in regulated industries
Hallucinations at scale: high-stakes errors in financial or legal contexts
No role-based access: same experience regardless of function

stakeholders.name: OpenAI
points: Commoditisation risk: Claude, Gemini narrowing the gap fast
Retention at depth: users adopt but don't always deepen usage
Monetisation ceiling: hard to upsell beyond Plus
Trust deficit in enterprise: compliance teams remain skeptical

questions.heading: What should a PM be asking?
questions.intro: Before designing any solution, these are the questions I'd want answered:
items: What does a user's session look like when they churn: What was the last interaction before they stopped returning?
Where does ChatGPT lose to competitors: Is it on depth, tone, accuracy, or something else entirely?
What percentage of users are using ChatGPT for high-stakes decisions: Financial, medical, relationship, career?
How often does ChatGPT validate an incorrect user assumption: And does the user ever realise it?
What would 'memory done right' look like: Beyond storing facts, how does ChatGPT build a behavioural model of the user over time?
What is the highest value enterprise use case that compliance teams would actually approve today?
How do we measure trust, not just engagement?
What does it mean for ChatGPT to be wise rather than just smart?

opportunities.title: Build a Discernment Layer
opportunities.description: Detect when a user is emotionally charged, overconfident, or moving too fast toward a consequential decision. Instead of validating, ChatGPT pauses, asks a clarifying question, and surfaces the assumption it thinks the user is making. The best thinking partners don't just agree - they challenge.

opportunities.title: Deep Memory Architecture
opportunities.description: Go beyond storing facts to building a true behavioural model. Not just what users tell it, but patterns it observes: how they decide, where they consistently hesitate, and what they need before they even know they need it. Smart is table stakes. The next ChatGPT needs to be wise.

metrics.heading: How would I measure success?
metrics.intro: If I shipped the Discernment Layer and Deep Memory Architecture, here's how I'd know it's working:

items.name: Decision Reversal Rate
items.description: How often users change course after a discernment prompt

items.name: Session Depth Score
items.description: Are conversations going deeper over time?

items.name: Memory Utilisation Rate
items.description: Are users engaging with recalled context?

items.name: Trust NPS
items.description: Do users trust ChatGPT more over time?

items.name: Discernment Acceptance Rate
items.description: Are users engaging with or dismissing discernment prompts?

items.name: Upgrade Conversion Rate
items.description: Does deeper value drive Plus subscriptions?

items.name: Compliance Approval Rate
items.description: % of use cases cleared by legal/risk

takeaway.heading: PM's Takeaway
takeaway.text: The one that started it all - breadth and personalisation are its superpowers, discernment is its blindspot, and wisdom is its next frontier.
